label: End-to-End Discovery to Concept
description: A product designer joins a new project cold and needs to move from zero domain knowledge to a validated concept direction. The path covers desk research, research planning, fieldwork, synthesis, framing, and concept ideation in a single coherent arc.

label: Lean Research Sprint for Concept Validation
description: A UX researcher running a time-boxed concept validation study designs the evaluation, builds personalised stimuli, runs moderated sessions, analyses findings, and shares highlights with stakeholders — all within a compressed sprint timeline.
stretch_flags: CON-025 → EXP-007: Concept UC followed by Explore UC — phase regression; intentional in a validation sprint context where stimuli are built before recruitment and screening

label: Product Designer Feature Design End-to-End
description: A mid-level product designer takes a feature from brief through wireframes, prototyping, visual QA, and handoff. The path reflects a solo practitioner doing cross-phase work on a well-scoped feature.

label: Visual Brand Direction for New Product
description: A brand or visual designer is tasked with establishing the visual language for a new product. They explore aesthetics through moodboarding, generate variants, remix references into a concept direction, critique it, and prepare a stakeholder walkthrough.

label: Design System Team Delivery Infrastructure
description: A design system lead builds the AI-ready infrastructure needed to make the team's system queryable by agents, then sets up governance pipelines and ongoing compliance monitoring to manage system health at scale.

label: Founder Zero-to-Prototype Without a Designer
description: A solo founder or PM needs to get to a testable product prototype quickly without design resource. They generate first-draft cross-functional screens, scaffold a prototype, get it in front of users in a research preview, and extract a functional specification for development.
stretch_flags: CON-070 → DEL-071: Research preview to functional spec extraction skips a formal validate step — intentional lean compression in a resource-constrained context

label: Research Operations Lead Building Knowledge Infrastructure
description: A senior researcher or research ops lead establishes durable team infrastructure: a project knowledge base seeded from prior research, prompt templates governing AI interaction quality, queryable personas for stakeholder use, and a repository that pushes relevant prior findings forward to new projects.
stretch_flags: DEF-012 → IMP-062: Persona generation (Define) to queryable personas (Improve) — real phase gap, but often collapsed in practice when a researcher builds both artifacts together

label: Design System Migration from Legacy Codebase
description: A design system engineer takes a product with no formal design system and extracts one from the existing codebase, then architects it for AI-readiness, sets up token management and linting, and configures self-healing automation for ongoing maintenance.

label: Continuous Improvement Loop Post-Launch
description: A product designer on a live product monitors user behaviour through VOC listening and session replay analysis, runs A/B experiments on identified friction points, and feeds outcomes back into the project knowledge base to inform the next design cycle.
stretch_flags: IMP-060 → VAL-036: A/B automation (Improve) followed by research bias audit (Validate) reverses typical phase order — defensible as a post-hoc quality check on experiment-derived conclusions

label: Longitudinal Qualitative Research Study
description: A senior UX researcher runs a diary study combined with follow-up interviews to understand evolving user behaviour over time. The path covers planning, fieldwork design, quality screening, synthesis, cross-study meta-analysis, and stakeholder delivery.
stretch_flags: DEF-018 → DEF-016: Cross-study meta-analysis feeds into session summarisation — a stretch; in practice these may run in parallel rather than sequentially

label: Designer-Led Code Contribution for Design Quality
description: A product designer working in a mature design-to-code pipeline uses system-grounded drafting to generate compliant components, validates them with linting and visual regression testing, then contributes design-quality fixes directly to the production codebase using an AI IDE.

label: Scenario-Driven Concept Development with Early Testing
description: A product designer uses narrative scenario generation to pressure-test a concept direction before committing to wireframes, then develops structured wireframes and a functional prototype, validates with simulated usability testing and attention analysis, and iterates before final handoff.
